collisions.paper: Defazio, 2025. Why Gradients Rapidly Increase Near the End of Training. arXiv:2506.02285
collisions.overlap: Establishes gradient-to-weight ratio steady state for normalized layers ('layer balancing'). Proposes AdamC with lambda_t proportional to gamma_t. This is the proportional/feedforward control component of the proposal.

collisions.paper: Chen et al., 2026. Cautious Weight Decay. ICLR 2026, arXiv:2510.12402
collisions.overlap: Binary sign-alignment mask for conditional weight decay. CWD's ablation (random mask vs sign-alignment) shows alignment structure matters. The proposal frames CWD as binary derivative control (K_d with binary alpha).

collisions.paper: Franke et al., 2024. Constrained Parameter Regularization. NeurIPS 2024, arXiv:2311.09058
collisions.overlap: Augmented Lagrangian per-matrix weight norm constraints with adaptive Lagrange multipliers and multiple kappa init strategies. Proposal frames as integral control (K_i > 0).

collisions.paper: Xie et al., 2023. Understanding and Scheduling Weight Decay. NeurIPS 2023
collisions.overlap: Gradient-norm-aware dynamic WD scheduling. Proposal frames as proportional control at gradient-norm-based setpoint.

collisions.paper: Kosson et al., 2024. Rotational Equilibrium: How Weight Decay Balances Learning Across Neural Networks. ICML 2024, arXiv:2305.17212
collisions.overlap: Analyzes rotational equilibrium under weight decay where angular updates converge to steady state across layers and neurons.

collisions.paper: Wang & Aitchison, 2025. How to set AdamW's weight decay as you scale model and dataset size. ICML 2025, arXiv:2405.13698
collisions.overlap: EMA timescale interpretation tau = 1/(eta*lambda) with constant optimal tau across scales. Basis for proposal's prescriptive rho*(t) = eta_t / tau.

collisions.paper: Chen et al., 2026. Decoupled Orthogonal Dynamics (AdamO). arXiv:2602.05136, Feb 2026
collisions.overlap: Identifies 'Radial Tug-of-War' where gradients push norm growth while WD suppresses it. Decouples radial (SGD) from tangential (Adam) updates. Conceptually related separation of norm control from directional learning.

collisions.paper: Holzl et al., 2025. Gradient-Weight Alignment as a Train-Time Proxy for Generalization. NeurIPS 2025, arXiv:2510.25480
collisions.overlap: Establishes gradient-weight alignment (GWA) as meaningful generalization signal. Supports H6 but uses alignment for monitoring, not WD modulation.

collisions.paper: PIDAO, 2024. Accelerated optimization with PID controller. Nature Communications
collisions.overlap: PID control framework for optimization, but applied to gradient step (optimizer), not WD coefficient design. Different application layer.

collisions.paper: He et al., 2025. AlphaDecay: Module-wise Weight Decay for Heavy-Tailed Balancing in LLMs. NeurIPS 2025, arXiv:2506.14562
collisions.overlap: Per-module adaptive WD using spectral properties. Different signal (heavy-tail alpha vs. gradient-to-weight ratio) and target domain (LLMs vs. vision).

collisions.paper: Andriushchenko, D'Angelo et al., 2024. Why Do We Need Weight Decay in Modern Deep Learning? NeurIPS 2024, arXiv:2310.04415
collisions.overlap: WD as dynamics modifier, not regularizer. Two roles: loss stabilization (vision) and bias-variance tradeoff (LLMs).

collisions.paper: Sun et al., 2025. Investigating the Role of Weight Decay in Enhancing Nonconvex SGD. CVPR 2025
collisions.overlap: Nonconvex SGDW convergence theory with generalization bounds. Proposal extends this with time-varying lambda_t and alignment modulation.

collisions.paper: Chou, 2025. Correction of Decoupled Weight Decay. arXiv:2512.08217
collisions.overlap: WD scaling as gamma^2 for stable norms. Orthogonal to alignment-based modulation.

collisions.paper: You et al. LARS (2017) / LAMB (2019). Layer-wise Adaptive Rate Scaling
collisions.overlap: Uses weight-norm-to-gradient-norm ratio ('trust ratio') per layer to scale learning rate. Inverse of proposal's gradient-to-weight ratio, used for LR scaling not WD modulation.

collisions.paper: Nakamura et al., 2019. Adaptive Weight Decay (AdaDecay). arXiv:1907.08931
collisions.overlap: Per-parameter adaptive WD proportional to normalized gradient magnitude per layer. Precursor to gradient-norm-based adaptive WD.

collisions.paper: arXiv:2510.19093, Oct 2025. Weight Decay May Matter More Than muP for Learning Rate Transfer
collisions.overlap: Unified framework connecting WD and muP through relative updates. WD stabilizes feature learning across widths.

candidates.differentiation_notes: The PID control law parameterization unifying CWD/SWD/CPR/Defazio/AdamWN is genuinely novel -- no prior work maps these methods to (K_p, K_i, K_d) gain configurations. The three metrics (BEM, CSI, AIS) are novel. The budget-efficiency theorem and batch-size-conditioned alignment recommendation are novel. AdamO (Feb 2026) must be discussed as concurrent work providing complementary norm control perspective. Dense related work (17+ papers 2019-2026) requires precise positioning.

collisions.paper: He et al., 2025. AlphaDecay: Module-wise Weight Decay for Heavy-Tailed Balancing in LLMs. NeurIPS 2025, arXiv:2506.14562
collisions.overlap: Also uses spectral properties of weight matrices for per-module WD, but uses static heavy-tail alpha exponent classification rather than dynamic condition number tracking.

collisions.paper: AAAI 2025. RG Framework for Scale-Invariant Feature Learning in Deep Neural Networks
collisions.overlap: Uses RG theory for feature learning analysis in DNNs. Different application (feature analysis vs. WD schedule design).

collisions.paper: Newhouse, 2025. Duality, Weight Decay, and Metrized Deep Learning. MIT Thesis
collisions.overlap: Connects spectral properties of weight matrices to optimization via duality framework.

candidates.differentiation_notes: Higher raw novelty due to fewer close prior works. Dynamic spectral condition number kappa(t) as WD schedule driver via RG flow derivation is genuinely novel. Must differentiate from AlphaDecay's static spectral approach. Execution risk: SVD cost at scale and empirical validation of non-monotone WD schedules.

collisions.paper: Hwang, 2024. FAdam: Adam is a natural gradient optimizer using diagonal empirical Fisher information. arXiv:2405.12807
collisions.overlap: CRITICAL: FAdam explicitly proposes Fisher-weighted natural-gradient WD -- 'weight decay should also be applied as a natural gradient' and 'components with low Fisher information can be pushed closer to zero.' This is conceptually the SAME core idea as cand_fisher_wd.

collisions.paper: Kirkpatrick et al., 2017. Elastic Weight Consolidation (EWC). PNAS
collisions.overlap: Uses diagonal Fisher information for per-parameter regularization strength in continual learning. Same core mechanism (Fisher-weighted per-parameter decay) but different application (preventing catastrophic forgetting vs. single-task generalization).

collisions.paper: AdaFisher, ICLR 2025. arXiv:2405.16397
collisions.overlap: Block-Kronecker Fisher approximation for adaptive preconditioning with integrated weight decay.

collisions.paper: arXiv:2507.18807, 2025. Fishers for Free? (Squisher)
collisions.overlap: Validates Adam's second moment as Fisher proxy for importance-weighted applications.

candidates.differentiation_notes: Initial novelty score of 9 was overly optimistic. FAdam (2024) substantially anticipates the core idea of Fisher-weighted WD. EWC (2017) establishes the Fisher-diagonal per-parameter decay mechanism. If pursued, must reframe as: (a) isolated ablation of FAdam's WD component, (b) systematic empirical study across architectures, or (c) James-Stein shrinkage connection as novel theoretical angle.

collisions.paper: Holzl et al., 2025. Gradient-Weight Alignment as a Train-Time Proxy for Generalization. NeurIPS 2025, arXiv:2510.25480
collisions.overlap: GWA already establishes that gradient-weight alignment carries generalization information. Substantially answers the core question of this candidate affirmatively, reducing novelty of the falsification framing.

collisions.paper: Chen et al., 2026. Cautious Weight Decay. ICLR 2026, arXiv:2510.12402
collisions.overlap: CWD ablations (random mask vs. sign-alignment mask) empirically demonstrate that alignment structure matters for WD effectiveness. Partially addresses the falsification question.

candidates.differentiation_notes: The standalone falsification study has reduced novelty after GWA (NeurIPS 2025) provided strong affirmative evidence for alignment informativeness. The temporal predictability gate, batch-size conditioning analysis, and AIS metric remain novel components but are better positioned as experiments within the unified framework (cand_udwdc) rather than a standalone paper.

key_risks: Dense related work landscape (17+ relevant papers 2019-2026) requires very precise positioning and extensive related work section
AdamO (Feb 2026) provides concurrent perspective on radial norm control -- must be discussed
FAdam (2024) substantially anticipates Fisher-weighted WD (impacts cand_fisher_wd, revised down from 9 to 6)
GWA (NeurIPS 2025) partially anticipates the alignment informativeness claims (H6, H7)
AlphaDecay (NeurIPS 2025) partially anticipates per-layer adaptive WD concept
The PID analogy must be shown to be prescriptive (not just descriptive) to avoid being seen as mere relabeling
recommendations: Proceed with cand_udwdc as front-runner; unification contribution is genuinely novel despite dense prior art
Integrate cand_falsification components (temporal predictability gate, batch-size sweep, AIS metric) into cand_udwdc rather than pursuing standalone
Deprioritize cand_fisher_wd due to FAdam collision; reframe if pursued
Keep cand_spectral_homeostatic as backup with highest raw novelty
Must cite and discuss AdamO (Feb 2026) as concurrent work on norm control
Expand related work section to cover all 17+ identified papers
Explicitly differentiate from PIDAO: PID for optimizer != PID for WD coefficient
Acknowledge GWA and frame H6/H7 as extending GWA to the WD coefficient design context
